frameworks.name: evidence accumulation in dynamic environments
frameworks.one_line: decisions formed by integrating noisy evidence with discounting of older samples

frameworks.name: provisional decision coding in frontal cortex
frameworks.one_line: FOF representation generalized from static to changing-state decisions

frameworks.name: behavioral model of changes of mind
frameworks.one_line: model-derived timing of mind-changes used to align neural activity

frameworks.name: temporal gain modulation
frameworks.one_line: evidence-encoding gain rises toward the action period

core_question: How does rat FOF represent accumulated evidence and provisional decisions when the environmental state can change during the trial?
key_assumptions: rats optimally discount older evidence in dynamic settings
provisional decisions are encoded in FOF firing rates
behavioral model predicts moment-by-moment changes of mind
manipulations: dynamic environment with switching state versus static decisions
trial epochs probing pre-action evidence integration
events identified as model-predicted changes of mind
key_findings: FOF encodes evidence throughout decision formation
encoding gain rises until the period when the rat may need to act
FOF neurons respond rapidly to changes of mind
firing rates track the new provisional decision after mind-changes
interpretations: FOF supports a stable representation of the current provisional choice even as it updates
evidence integration and choice updating are dissociable and both observable in FOF
